steps.title: Spot the defining contrast
steps.content: Sir Winston looks impressive—features suited for a bust, forehead bulging with apparent genius—yet the narrator sarcastically questions his “actual achievements” after fifty years of service. This sarcasm reveals the core contrast: impressive appearance versus uncertain or unproven accomplishment.

steps.title: Match the contrast to the choices
steps.content:
C directly captures this: appearance of distinction paired with uncertainty about actual accomplishment.

The other three either invent details (A's dishonorable actions), contradict the passage (B's modest profile), or misread the sarcasm as evidence of hidden achievements (D).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
